The girl that the sicario wants to save doesn't seem like she'll cut it in this sense, and, yes, as others have pointed out, this situation is a bit ironic considering he executed the kids in the first flick (maybe the point here will be that he's "changing" from a hell bent assassin to a "good" guy (?), but that does seem a bit ham-fisted and typically-Hollywood-predictable plot-wise)...anyway, we shall see. I'll def. drop the coin to see this in the theater regardless.

+Arpan Ghosh Well this isn't going to be the same director (the first movie's director being Denis Villeneuve) which automatically lowers my expectations. And they are also blatantly just re-using the same soundtrack from the first movie, which is plain lazy. Not only that but they're also pulling the "Mexico" card again, as well as using the age-old "Father-Daughter" dynamic that we've seen so many times before. What was great about the first film was how original and unique it was in it's story, style, soundtrack, and cinematography. They didn't set up the first film for there to be a sequel, but they made one anyways because they know it will make money. I still plan on seeing this because I remember the trailer for the first movie also seeming like a generic action flick, when it wasn't. And I don't hate the whole thing. There are actually a lot of things in this trailer I like, like the aesthetic of the skulls when giving the Josh Brolin and Benicio Del Toro credits, as well as the soundtrack in the second half of the trailer. Overall I have low expectations for what believe to be very valid reasons. I think you're entitled to your own opinion, as everyone should be, and I 'm glad that you liked the trailer.

Benicio Del Toro as the protagonist? Why? He was pretty much the villain in the first film. 

A man who would do absolutely anything to exact horrifying, brutal vengeance upon the other terrible person who wronged him (this is an understatement). How does it make any sense to portray him as this hard, badass antihero who at his heart has good intentions? He's cruel and traded his humanity for bloody revenge because he had nothing left to lose. 

I'm not saying the movie won't be good, hell, maybe it'll be fantastic like the first. I just don't know how I feel about the direction they're choosing to go with. Also, this is a trailer, and I know that trailers can misrepresent a film and make it seem less intelligent than it really is. Just watch the trailer for Children of Men and you'll know what I mean. 

Also, "Sicario, Day of the Soldado" is an awful title. "Soldado," has a perfect ring to it, just like Sicario. Plus, it very literally reflects the shift of Del Toro's character from hitman to soldier. "Day of the Soldado" is a mouthful and confusing.
